senses.definition: to impress someone greatly

examples.x: The dance routine blew away the judges on the competition.
The unexpected talent of the street performer blew away onlookers.
The energetic performance of the band blew away the crowd.

senses.definition: to talk without a pause

examples.x: As they waited in line, the friends chattered away, sharing jokes and stories.
Even during the long journey, he chattered away, making the time pass quickly.

senses.definition: to stop using or having something

examples.x: In an effort to reduce waste, the company decided to do away with single-use plastic in its packaging.
The government planned to do away with outdated regulations that hindered economic growth.
The school board voted to do away with the traditional grading system in favor of a more comprehensive approach.
The organization aimed to do away with inefficient processes to improve productivity.
As part of the cost-cutting measures, the company chose to do away with certain non-essential services.

senses.definition: to provide reasons or justifications in an attempt to dismiss or minimize the significance of something

examples.x: The politician tried to explain away the scandal, but the public demanded more accountability.
The manager explained away the poor sales figures by blaming the bad weather and economic downturn.
The company's statement aimed to explain away the environmental concerns raised by activists, but the community remained skeptical.

senses.definition: to slowly and carelessly waste or use up something, such as time, money, resources, or opportunities

examples.x: He tends to fritter away his weekends by watching TV and playing video games.
Don't fritter away your savings on unnecessary expenses; save for the future.
Frittering away our limited resources on trivial matters is not a wise strategy.

senses.definition: to start talking about something that is different from the topic of the discussion

examples.x: During the meeting, he tried to get away from the budget issues and discuss potential marketing strategies.
The teacher encouraged students to get away from the assigned reading and share their personal experiences related to the topic.
It's easy for him to get away from the main point of the conversation by bringing up unrelated stories.
The speaker frequently got away from the main theme of the lecture, leading to some confusion among the audience.
In a debate, it's important to stick to the topic and not get away from the core arguments.

senses.definition: to give something as a gift or donation to someone

examples.x: She decided to give away her old clothes to a local charity.
The school organized a book drive to give away textbooks to underprivileged students.
The company is going to give away free samples of their new product at the event.

senses.definition: to prevent somebody or something from accessing a particular place or area
to put a person in a place where they can not escape from, such as a psychiatric hospital or prison

examples.x: The authorities locked away the dangerous criminal in a maximum-security prison.
The patient was locked away in a psychiatric hospital for observation.
The court ordered the individual to be locked away in a psychiatric hospital for treatment.
The authorities locked away the suspects in separate cells pending further investigation.
The parents struggled with the decision to lock away their troubled child for their own safety and well-being.

senses.definition: to no longer be alive

examples.x: The community mourned when they heard that the beloved teacher had passed away.
I just found out that my childhood friend passed away in an accident.
She passed away peacefully in her sleep.
We were saddened to hear that the town's oldest resident passed away at the age of 105.
My grandfather passed away last year after a long illness.

senses.definition: to move or back away from someone or something, often suddenly or quickly

examples.x: She pulled away when he tried to hug her.
The cat pulled away as I reached out to pet it.
As their argument intensified, he tried to console her, but she pulled away.

senses.definition: to steal something and escape without being caught

examples.x: The gang of robbers managed to run away with a large sum of cash from the bank heist.
The cunning thief managed to run away with the valuable jewels from the museum.
The master jewel thief ran away with the famous Pink Diamond, leaving no trace behind.
The ingenious hacker managed to run away with confidential data from a highly secured government server.
The sly pickpocket successfully ran away with the tourist's wallet in the crowded marketplace.

senses.definition: to frighten someone so much

examples.x: His angry outburst scared away the customers from the store.
The uncertainty surrounding the project is scaring away potential collaborators.
The constant delays in delivery are scaring away customers from ordering online.

senses.definition: to request or order something from an organization by sending them a written or online inquiry

examples.x: She sent away for a free catalog from the clothing store.
He sent away for a travel guide to plan his vacation.
She decided to send away for a free sample of the new skincare product advertised on television.
Before the internet, people used to send away for catalogs to browse and order products from their favorite retailers.
The child eagerly sent away for a mail-order toy using the money saved from their allowance.

senses.definition: (of an area or land) to extend over a considerable distance

examples.x: The vast desert landscape stretched away as far as the eye could see.
As we stood on the beach, the ocean stretched away to the horizon.
The endless meadow stretched away, with wildflowers dotting the landscape.
From the mountaintop, the valleys below stretched away in all directions.

senses.definition: to take something from someone so that they no longer have it

examples.x: The police took away the suspect's illegal items.
The company will take away your access card if you violate the security policy.
The security guard took away entry rights for individuals without proper identification.
The administrator took away the student's access to online resources for misconduct.

senses.definition: to spend time in a relaxed manner, often without a specific purpose

examples.x: The old couple enjoys whiling away their retirement years in a peaceful town.
Let's while away the time with a relaxing spa day.

senses.definition: to slowly reduce the value, size, etc. of something

examples.x: Continuous lies and deceit can whittle away the trust between friends, leading to a strained relationship.
Poor financial decisions can whittle away a person's savings, leaving them with less money for emergencies or future plans.
Industrial activities that release pollutants can whittle away the quality of air and water in a region, impacting the environment over the long term.
Unhealthy habits, such as a poor diet and lack of exercise, can slowly whittle away one's physical well-being, leading to various health issues.
Persistent dissatisfaction at work can whittle away an employee's motivation and morale, affecting their overall job satisfaction.
Economic downturns often whittle away the value of investments.
The erosion process gradually whittled away the coastline.

senses.definition: to gradually decrease and become less intense or smaller in amount

examples.x: Her laughter began to die away as she realized the seriousness of the situation.
The music slowly died away as the night progressed.
The enthusiasm for the project never died away among the team.
The excitement did not die away despite the challenges.

senses.definition: to slowly remove or destroy something over time

examples.x: Over time, the rust began to eat away at the metal bridge, causing structural concerns.
Neglecting dental hygiene can allow plaque to eat away at tooth enamel, leading to cavities.
The constant exposure to harsh weather conditions started to eat away at the exterior paint of the house.
Procrastination can eat away at your time, making it challenging to meet deadlines.
Unresolved conflicts within a relationship can eat away at trust and intimacy.
The frequent use of harsh chemicals can eat away at the protective layer of the skin.

senses.definition: to gradually lose intensity or strength

examples.x: The excitement among the crowd began to fall away as the event dragged on.
As the storm moved away, the wind and rain started to fall away.
The noise from the street began to fall away as we moved further indoors.
The scent of the flowers will gradually fall away as the blooms start to fade.
The sunlight began to fall away as the evening approached, casting longer shadows.

senses.definition: to consume a large quantity of food

examples.x: I can't believe how much pizza he can pack away.
She packed away three servings of spaghetti at dinner.
He was so hungry, he packed away the whole sandwich in minutes.

senses.definition: to eat a large amount of food quickly

examples.x: He was so hungry, he put away the entire pizza in minutes.
After the marathon, she put away a huge meal.
can't believe how much food he can put away!

senses.definition: to go to a secluded place to avoid being found by others

examples.x: He decided to hide away in his room to get some quiet and privacy.
When she's upset, she likes to hide away in her room and have some quiet time.
During the storm, the children decided to hide away in the basement for safety.
The reclusive author would often hide away in a remote cabin to work on his novels in solitude.
He wanted to hide away in the library, away from the bustling city, to concentrate on his studies.

senses.definition: to secretly store something in a place in order to use it later

examples.x: The pirate captain stashed away the treasure on a remote island.
He stashed away his favorite snacks so his siblings wouldn't find them.
The detective carefully stashed away the evidence to ensure its confidentiality.

senses.definition: to hide oneself on a vehicle or vessel, such as a ship, airplane, or train, without permission or payment of fare

examples.x: The curious teenager attempted to stow away on the circus train to experience life under the big top.
Despite strict security measures, someone managed to stow away on the military transport plane.
Seeking adventure, the young explorers decided to stow away on a pirate ship in their favorite story.
The spy successfully stowed away in the diplomatic convoy to gather information on the summit.
During the border crossing, a group of migrants attempted to stow away on a freight train headed north.

senses.definition: to work tirelessly and energetically on a particular task or project

examples.x: The students were determined to beaver away on their group project until it was perfect.
Despite feeling tired, she continued to beaver away at the challenging puzzle.
The dedicated employees beavered away in the office late into the night.
The students were determined to beaver away throughout the semester to excel in their exams.
The writer spent months beavering away on the novel, crafting each sentence with precision.

senses.definition: to make a great and persistent effort in order to accomplish or resolve a task or problem

examples.x: He continued to hammer away at his research project until he found a breakthrough.
Despite the challenges, she kept hammering away at her novel until it was finally published.
The team will hammer away at the issue until they reach a solution.
They hammered away at the construction project, working long hours to meet the deadline.
He hammered away at the puzzle until he solved it.

senses.definition: to work hard and persistently for a long time to get a job done or reach a goal

examples.x: The dedicated team slaved away on the new software development until the early hours of the morning.
She's been slaving away at her novel, hoping to complete it by the end of the month.
The students slaved away on their research papers during the study weekend.
They've been slaving away in the kitchen to prepare a special meal for the guests.
The artist has been slaving away on the painting, striving to capture every detail with precision.

senses.definition: to keep working hard, even when faced with difficulties or challenges

examples.x: I've been plugging away at this novel for months, and it's almost finished.
While others gave up, she plugged away and eventually succeeded.
They plugged away at the project until it was finally complete.
Even when things got tough, she continued to plug away at her goals.
Despite the challenges, he plugged away and achieved top grades.

senses.definition: to work persistently, often for an extended period and to achieve a goal or complete a task

examples.x: The team slogged away on the construction site to meet the tight deadline.
He has been slogging away at his novel, hoping to finish it by the end of the month.
Despite facing challenges, she continued to slog away at her research project.
The students slogged away at their group project, meeting late into the night to finish on time.
Undeterred by setbacks, she continued to slog away at her fitness routine, determined to reach her goals.
